name: John McLaughlin
description: English guitarist, founder of the Mahavishnu Orchestra
abstract: John McLaughlin (born 4 January 1942), also previously known as Mahavishnu, is an English guitarist, bandleader, and composer. A pioneer of jazz fusion, his music combines elements of jazz with rock, world music, Western classical music, flamenco, and blues.

concerts.label: It's a Beautiful Day, Psychedelic/Folk Rock Band

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: The Five Peace Band was just my love for my friend John McLaughlin... have been friends through the years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: It was me and Chick, John McLaughlin, Albert Mangelsdorff on trombone... and Jean-Luc Ponty... Joachim Berendt... said, 'Great idea—why don't you all play something together as a jam session at the end'
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I was always fascinated with Tony Williams’ Lifetime band. That band was light-years ahead of a lot of organ trios, with Larry Young and John McLaughlin.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ran Blake
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] That’s certainly something for those moments. I had one where I was at a Mahavishnu Orchestra concert with John McLaughlin and Billy Cobham did something on the drums that made me like leap out of my seat. And I’m not prone to do that kind of thing. [INTERVIEWEE] And Billy of course.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ran Blake interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 16, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Paquito D'Rivera
about_quotes.evidence: I have to confess that that group didn’t impress nobody there... or the trio that they did with John McLaughlin, the Trio of Doom. There was something really, nobody for us, but the funny for us, something that didn’t impress us at all
about_quotes.doc_title: Paquito D'Rivera — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Levin
about_quotes.evidence: I went to — I remember the concert but I don’t remember who was headlining it, but John McLaughlin was the opening act... And he was playing the most amazing stuff... And I started to track down his stuff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Levin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001
